_docs.purpose: Single source of truth for the CLI-visible knob surface: every config key, dotted config path, CLI flag, embedder (napi/engine request) field, and external-tool flag that a zzop message is allowed to name. Consumed by lib/mapper.js (KNOWN_KEYS, runtime drift warnings) AND by packages/engine/tests/rule_contracts.rs's reference-validation meta-test (CHECK A / CHECK B), so both sides of the contract read the exact same data — a config key or flag can never drift between what the CLI accepts and what the engine's own rule/warning messages are allowed to recommend.
_docs.configKeys: Moved verbatim from lib/mapper.js's former KNOWN_KEYS constant — the fixed-shape config object's known keys, by scope. See mapper.js's own comment for the 'warn on drift, never reject' contract this backs.
_docs.configPaths: Every valid DOTTED spelling of a nested config key (derived from configKeys), in the form an author would actually write it in a message: `<parent>.<child>` for a fixed-shape nested object (e.g. `git.since`), `<parent>[].<child>` for a per-entry field of an array-of-objects (e.g. `trees[].root`). Deliberately excludes `rules.<id>.severity`/`rules.<id>.exclude`: `<id>` is an open-keyed rule id, not enumerable here (a message naming a specific rule id under `rules` is checked as a JSON-snippet shape instead, see rule_contracts.rs's CHECK B doc).
_docs.cliFlags: The real flags packages/cli/bin/zzop.js's parseArgs() recognizes, enumerated by reading that file (not assumed) — both the long and short spelling where both exist. `--force` is init-only, `--mode`/`--kind` are init-adapter-only, every other flag is run-only (see parseArgs' scoped-flag cross-check).
_docs.embedderFields: napi/engine request-surface field names (packages/napi/src/api.rs's AnalyzeRequest / packages/napi/index.js) that legitimately appear in a zzop message as 'the embedder dialect' — i.e. the field name a HOST APPLICATION embedding @zzop/native directly (not through the zzop CLI/config file) would set. Every native rule's disable-hint already says this explicitly: 'Disable via config `rules: {...}` (embedders: `disabled_rules`)'. Kept to exactly the fields a message in the scanned dirs actually references today (see rule_contracts.rs's CHECK B doc comment for the verification) — extend only when a new message legitimately needs to name another embedder field, not preemptively.
_docs.externalToolFlags: Flags of OTHER tools (never zzop's own) that a zzop message legitimately mentions when explaining an OUTSIDE cause for a symptom. Today that's exactly git's own `--depth` (a shallow clone, e.g. `git clone --depth=1`) and `--unshallow` (`git fetch --unshallow`, the fix) — both from packages/metrics/src/diagnostics.rs's thin-git-history warning. Kept minimal: enumerated by scanning the real tree, not pre-populated with every flag a tool happens to have.
_docs.allowlistedTokens: Literal tokens that are not config paths/keys at all but legitimately sit inside CHECK B's +/-120-char 'near the word config' window anyway — a filename convention (`zzop.config.jsonc`, the CLI's own config file, referenced as plain prose text today but allowlisted preemptively so a future backtick-quoted mention doesn't spuriously fail) or an unrelated identifier/header name that happens to share a sentence with a config-disable hint. Each entry's source is documented in rule_contracts.rs's CHECK B doc comment, not just here.
